title: Cinderwatch Gate — missing patrol / notice-board pressure
notes: Canonical long-session spine for offline validation; frontier_gate diegesis.

continuity_anchors.description: Gate serjeant, tavern runner, threadbare watcher, hooded lurker, noble townhouse colors.
Posted warning and whispers about a missing patrol; rising crowd tension.
Cinderwatch Gate District — rain, choke traffic, notice board, tavern heat at the edge.
Learn what the watch will admit, what the notice omits, and where the patrol rumor points.

referent_anchors.label: Ash Compact census pressure
referent_anchors.description: Faction/project subtext: supply census lines and gate delays tied to compact policy.

referent_anchors.label: Captain Thoran
referent_anchors.description: Named watch officer referenced for patrol assignments and roster truth.

referent_anchors.label: Muddy footprints clue
referent_anchors.description: Discovered lead: faint prints northwest among crates; hurried movement.

referent_anchors.label: The notice
referent_anchors.description: Posted taxes, curfews, and the missing-patrol warning at the board.

progression_anchors.description: Patrol disappearance should deepen: named routes, last sightings, or clock pressure.
Watch posture should harden if the player presses publically or spikes panic.

checkpoints.label: Notice facts anchored before social escalation
Patrol thread tied to clue + progression expectation
NPC cast + compact referent in play

branches.label: Cautious observation / delayed commitment
branches.notes: Hold position, map rotations, defer confrontation.

turns.player_prompt: I loiter in rain shadow and count guard rotations.
I map who watches the notice board.
I wait a full bell before acting.
I feign fatigue near the tavern door, listening only.
I ignore provocations and track hand signals in the crowd.
I note where the well-dressed observer drifts.
I watch the gate without approaching yet.
I commit to returning after dark; I withdraw cleanly.
I pocket a rumor silently and leave the square.
I file a mental timeline: arrivals versus shouts.

branches.label: Direct action / risky intrusion
branches.notes: Force doors, confront watchers, spike gate tension.

turns.player_prompt: I slip toward the narrow alley without announcing myself.
I try the warehouse side door when guards look away.
I palm the notice edge to read what is pasted behind it.
I confront the threadbare watcher aloud.
I press through mud toward the gate serjeant.
I shout a blunt question about the missing patrol.
I offer a low bribe for duty-roster facts.
I sprint for the crates when a hooded figure moves.

branches.label: Investigation / social inquiry
branches.notes: Long path: notice, runners, watch names, compact subtext.

turns.player_prompt: I scan the notice for taxes, curfew, and patrol lines.
I ask a guard who commands the watch tonight.
I greet the tavern runner and ask the loudest rumor.
I mention the missing patrol and watch their face.
I ask who posted the warning beside the tax lines.
I press: what route did the patrol take last?
I ask after Lirael the crier and where she posts.
I inquire politely after Captain Thoran.
I listen to refugees whispering at the mud line.
I ask if Ash Compact census lines caused the choke.
I note the threadbare pickpocket and avoid eye contact.
I ask the serjeant what changed at the gate this week.
I request the exact safe-passage wording on the board.
I ask whether hooded figures are common here.
I buy a cup and trade a coin for a straight rumor.
I ask about the noble colors on the townhouse.
I mention muddy alleys; did patrols search northwest?
I ask who benefits if the gate stays jammed.
I confirm curfew bells with the watch.
I stake out the board a few minutes, reading edits.
I ask about the eastern caravan choke point.
I probe whether taxes rose after the census.
I thank the runner and ask one last sharp question.
I summarize what I heard and ask what I missed.
I state my next focus: follow the footprint rumor.
